seoTitle: How to Set Autoplay for Videos in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to set the autoplay option for videos in Canva so clips start playing automatically without a manual click.
intro: Autoplay lets your video content start immediately when a presentation or design loads, which can improve viewer engagement in marketing or social content. This guide covers enabling that setting on a video element in Canva.

steps.heading: Select the video element
steps.body: Click the video clip you want to configure so its playback options appear.

steps.heading: Open the video settings menu
steps.body: Look for a settings or 'more options' icon near the video toolbar.

steps.heading: Enable the autoplay toggle
steps.body: Switch on the autoplay option so the video starts without requiring a click.

steps.heading: Check mute settings
steps.body: Many platforms require autoplay videos to start muted, so confirm this setting matches your needs.

steps.heading: Preview and publish
steps.body: Test the design in preview or present mode to confirm the video starts automatically as expected.

faq.question: Does autoplay work the same way on every platform I share to?
faq.answer: Not always — some platforms restrict autoplay unless the video is muted, so check playback after publishing.

faq.question: Can I turn off autoplay later if I change my mind?
faq.answer: Yes, simply toggle the setting back off in the video element's options.

faq.question: Is autoplay available for free Canva users?
faq.answer: Yes, autoplay settings are part of the standard video editing tools.

faq.question: Will autoplay work in a downloaded video file?
faq.answer: Autoplay settings mainly apply to how a Canva design plays within presentations or shared links, not standalone exported video files.
